A deeply fused CUDA kernel that co-optimizes the entire SwiGLU MLP forward pass (gate projection → SiLU activation → elementwise multiply → down projection) to minimize HBM traffic and maximize L1/L2 cache reuse during memory-bound LLM inference. In long-context agentic LLM inference, token generation is memory-bandwidth bottleneck rather than compute-bound. Useful for: Long-context agentic inference (RAG, code generation, multi-turn dialogue) where batch size is small (1-8) and generation length is high
Deep dive: Full analysis

10. Differentially Private and Communication Efficient Large Language Model Split Inference via Stochastic Quantization and Soft Prompt
Summary:
Reduces communication overhead and preserves privacy in LLM split inference by projecting and quantizing token embeddings with differential privacy guarantees, while using server-side soft prompts to recover utility without requiring local denoising models. Existing privacy-preserving LLM split inference methods require transmitting high-dimensional token embeddings and deploying resource-intensive local denoising models, creating excessive communication and computation overhead that prevents deployment on resource-constrained devices. Useful for: Resource-constrained edge devices requiring private LLM inference (mobile, IoT)
Deep dive: Full analysis

11. Towards Personalized Bangla Book Recommendation: A Large-Scale Multi-Entity Book Graph Dataset
Summary:
A large-scale heterogeneous knowledge graph dataset for Bangla book recommendations that connects books, users, authors, categories, publishers, and reviews through eight relation types to enable graph-based and hybrid recommendation models in a low-resource language setting. Personalized book recommendation in Bangla literature has been severely limited by the lack of structured, large-scale, publicly available datasets. Useful for: Low-resource language recommendation systems (cultural/regional content platforms)
Deep dive: Full analysis

12. Where Bits Matter in World Model Planning: A Paired Mixed-Bit Study for Efficient Spatial Reasoning
Summary:
Allocate quantization bits strategically across world model modules (encoder, dynamics, decoder) rather than uniformly, preserving encoder precision to maintain spatial reasoning quality in the 4-bit transition regime. Uniform low-bit quantization (e.g., INT4) causes catastrophic collapse in world model planning performance, but blindly increasing bits everywhere is computationally wasteful. Useful for: Real-time robotics planning under memory/compute constraints (embedded vision-based navigation)
Deep dive: Full analysis

Cross-Paper Insights
• Papers #4, #10 share technique: based on the paper text

digest/2026-02-14.title: Research Digest — Sat 2026-02-14
digest/2026-02-14.content:
Research Digest — Sat 2026-02-14
Scanned 20 papers • Selected 3 • Filtered 1 off-domain, 12 duplicates • Runtime: 2.0 min

Papers





























#PaperTagsLinks1Semantic Search At LinkedIn, LLM-Driven Autonomous Optimization for Industrial-Scale Recommendation Systems, and More!Embeddings, Diffusion, LLM, SearchLink • Note2MemFly: On-the-Fly Memory Optimization via Information BottleneckRecSys, LLM, RAGarXiv • Note3Dissecting Subjectivity and the”Ground Truth”Illusion in Data AnnotationSearcharXiv • Note

Details
1. Semantic Search At LinkedIn, LLM-Driven Autonomous Optimization for Industrial-Scale Recommendation Systems, and More!
Source: RecSys Substack | Tags: Embeddings, Diffusion, LLM, Search, RAG, RecSys
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Deploy large language models as rerankers in production search systems by using a two-stage retrieval architecture where fast embedding-based retrieval narrows candidates before LLM-based scoring. Traditional semantic search relies on embedding similarity (e.g., cosine distance) which captures topical relevance but fails to model nuanced ranking signals like query intent, freshness, personalization, and multi-faceted relevance that require reasoning over multiple signals simultaneously. Useful for: Large-scale search systems (LinkedIn Jobs, Google Search, e-commerce product search)
Vault connections: A Survey of Large Language Model Empowered Agents for Recommendation and Search: Towards Next-Generation Information Retrieval, Knowledge Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation for LLM-based Recommendation, Generative Large Recommendation Model: Emerging Trends in LLM for Recommendation

2. MemFly: On-the-Fly Memory Optimization via Information Bottleneck
Source: HuggingFace | Tags: RecSys, LLM, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Applies information bottleneck principles to dynamically compress LLM agent memory by minimizing compression entropy while maximizing task-relevant entropy, creating a stratified memory structure with hybrid semantic-symbolic-topological retrieval. Existing LLM memory frameworks struggle to balance efficient compression of redundant historical interactions with maintaining precise retrieval for complex downstream tasks, leading to either bloated context windows or loss of critical information. Useful for: Long-horizon agentic tasks requiring coherent memory over hundreds of interactions (customer support, personal assistants)
Vault connections: MemoryBank, Hierarchical Agent Memory for Contextual Recommendation, Episodic Memory with Ebbinghaus Forgetting

3. Dissecting Subjectivity and the”Ground Truth”Illusion in Data Annotation
Source: S2:NeurIPS | Tags: Search
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Replace consensus-seeking annotation pipelines with systems that explicitly capture, preserve, and model legitimate disagreement as a signal of cultural and perspectival diversity rather than noise to be resolved. Traditional “ground truth” paradigms treat annotator disagreement as random error to be minimized through majority voting or expert adjudication, systematically erasing minority perspectives and cultural variation. Useful for: Subjective NLP tasks (hate speech, toxicity, sentiment, offensiveness detection)
Vault connections: Perception and Skepticism, Ontological Structure of Aristotelian Logic, Modeling Open-World Cognition as On-Demand Synthesis of Probabilistic Model


🎯 Active Projects
Cross-Paper Insights
• Vault gaps: limited coverage of Dissecting Subjectivity and the”Ground Truth”Illus

digest/2026-02-15.title: Research Digest — Sun 2026-02-15
digest/2026-02-15.content:
Research Digest — Sun 2026-02-15
Scanned 15 papers • Selected 1 • Filtered 2 off-domain, 7 duplicates • Runtime: 2.6 min

Papers

















#PaperTagsLinks1Amsterdam Compiler KitSearchLink • Code • Note

Details
1. Amsterdam Compiler Kit
Source: HackerNews | Tags: Search
Code: github.com/davidgiven/ack
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A portable, retargetable compiler infrastructure using an intermediate representation (EM bytecode) that decouples front-ends (language parsers) from back-ends (target machine code generators), enabling write-once compile-anywhere toolchains. - N×M compiler problem: Building compilers for N languages and M architectures traditionally requires N×M implementations

Code portability: Programs written once should run on diverse hardware without rewriting
Compiler construction overhead: Writing machine-specific optimizers and code generators for each language/platform pair Useful for: Cross-platform development: Building software for embedded systems, microcomputers, and diverse CPU architectures from a single codebase

Vault connections: Maximizing Claude Code with reusable workflows, 12 Factor Agent (Talk), Code Report • July 16 2025


🎯 Active Projects
Cross-Paper Insights

digest/2026-02-16.title: Research Digest — Mon 2026-02-16
digest/2026-02-16.content:
Research Digest — Mon 2026-02-16
Scanned 291 papers • Selected 8 • Filtered 2 off-domain, 32 duplicates • Runtime: 8.6 min

Papers

Details
1. LCSB: Layer-Cyclic Selective Backpropagation for Memory-Efficient On-Device LLM Fine-Tuning
Source: arXiv:cs.CL | Tags: LLM, Transformer
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Compute gradients for only a subset of transformer layers per training step while relying on residual connections for gradient flow and AdamW momentum for implicit updates to non-selected layers. Memory-efficient backpropagation (MeBP) enables on-device LLM fine-tuning but still requires backward computation through all layers at every step, with weight decompression consuming 32-42% of backward time—a critical bottleneck for mobile devices with <1GB memory. Useful for: On-device LLM fine-tuning on mobile/edge devices with severe memory constraints (<1GB)
Vault connections: Native Sparse Attention (NSA): Summary, Early-Exit Large Language Models, Efficient Attention Mechanisms for Large Language Model: A Survey

2. RQ-GMM: Residual Quantized Gaussian Mixture Model for Multimodal Semantic Discretization in CTR Prediction
Source: arXiv:cs.IR | Tags: Embeddings, RecSys
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Uses Gaussian Mixture Models combined with residual quantization to discretize multimodal embeddings into semantic IDs, modeling the probabilistic structure of embedding spaces for improved codebook utilization and reconstruction accuracy in CTR prediction. Addresses three limitations of semantic discretization for multimodal embeddings: (1) poor codebook utilization (many codes unused), (2) low reconstruction accuracy when mapping continuous embeddings to discrete IDs, and (3) insufficient semantic discriminability between similar items. Useful for: Click-through rate prediction with multimodal content (video, image, text)
Vault connections: U-MARVEL: Main Takeaways and Techniques for Practitioners, Semantic Code Quantization Fusion, Multimodal Embedding Clustering and Behavioral Alignment (Kuaishou M3CSR)

3. Look Inward to Explore Outward: Learning Temperature Policy from LLM Internal States via Hierarchical RL
Source: arXiv:cs.CL | Tags: LLM, RL
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that learns to dynamically control sampling temperature at each decoding step by conditioning on the LLM’s hidden states, jointly optimizing both temperature selection and token generation from downstream task rewards. Existing RLVR methods use static temperature values or heuristic schedules that are decoupled from task rewards, leading to suboptimal exploration-exploitation trade-offs during trajectory sampling for policy learning. Useful for: Mathematical reasoning tasks requiring adaptive exploration (proof search, multi-step problem solving)
Vault connections: Reinforcement learning in LLM – recent turbulence, Self-Evolved Reward Learning (SER), Policy Gradients

4. Unified Multi-Domain Graph Pre-training for Homogeneous and Heterogeneous Graphs via Domain-Specific Expert Encoding
Source: arXiv:cs.LG | Tags: None
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A unified multi-domain graph pre-training framework that trains domain-specific experts independently on both homogeneous and heterogeneous graphs, then adaptively fuses them for downstream tasks to handle cross-graph-type and cross-domain distribution shifts. Existing graph pre-training methods are tailored to either homogeneous or heterogeneous graphs exclusively, failing to leverage mixed graph types during pre-training and struggling with distribution shifts when the upstream pre-training graph type differs from downstream deployment graphs. Useful for: Cross-domain graph transfer learning where training and test graphs have different structures (e.g., molecular graphs → social networks)
Vault connections: Dual-Oriented Graph-Based Contrastive Framework for CDR, HSAL-GNN Forward Propagation, Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network with Quadrant-Aware Propagation

5. Closing the Loop: A Control-Theoretic Framework for Provably Stable Time Series Forecasting with LLMs
Source: arXiv:cs.LG | Tags: LLM, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A closed-loop control-theoretic framework that stabilizes autoregressive time series forecasting by actively correcting prediction drift through a learnable residual estimator (Observer) and feedback controller. Addresses error accumulation (exposure bias) in standard autoregressive LLM forecasting where models operate open-loop, recursively consuming their own predictions and causing minor early deviations to cascade into significant trajectory drift over long horizons. Useful for: Long-horizon time series forecasting where error accumulation is critical (financial markets, weather prediction)
Vault connections: Reasoning Beyond Limits: Advances and Open Problems for LLMs, Recursive Language Models, Large Language Models Can Solve Real-World Planning Rigorously with Formal Verification Tools

6. R-Diverse: Mitigating Diversity Illusion in Self-Play LLM Training
Source: arXiv:cs.LG | Tags: LLM, RAG
Code: github.com/Gengsheng-Li/R-Diverse
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Sustain self-play LLM training by preventing diversity illusion through a persistent memory bank that discourages cross-iteration pattern recycling (MAP) and a skill-based diversity metric that evaluates questions by reasoning skills required rather than surface variation (SAM). Existing self-play frameworks (e.g., R-Zero) suffer from non-sustained improvement where early gains degrade as training continues, caused by (1) Local Diversity Illusion—within-batch diversity enforcement that induces cross-iteration mode cycling, and (2) Surface Diversity Illusion—superficially varied questions that exercise identical underlying reasoning skills. Useful for: Self-supervised bootstrapping of reasoning capabilities in LLMs without large-scale human annotation
Vault connections: Reasoning Beyond Limits: Advances and Open Problems for LLMs, Mathematical Problem-Solving Synthetic Query Generation, Hybrid Demonstration Mining (Self + External)

7. TRACE: Temporal Reasoning via Agentic Context Evolution for Streaming Electronic Health Records (EHRs)
Source: arXiv:cs.AI | Tags: LLM, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A framework that enables frozen LLMs to perform temporal clinical reasoning over longitudinal patient data by maintaining a dual-memory architecture (static Global Protocol + dynamic Individual Protocol) coordinated by four specialized agentic components instead of extending context windows or fine-tuning. LLMs struggle with longitudinal patient trajectories where evolving clinical states, irregular timing, and heterogeneous events cause performance degradation over time; existing solutions (fine-tuning, RAG) introduce computational overhead, privacy constraints, or instability under long contexts. Useful for: Longitudinal clinical decision support over streaming EHR data
Vault connections: Mind-Map Agent, In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL), A Survey of Context Engineering for Large Language Model

8. TensorCommitments: A Lightweight Verifiable Inference for Language Models
Source: arXiv:cs.AI | Tags: LLM
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A tensor-native cryptographic commitment scheme that binds LLM inference to an irreversible tag organized in multivariate Terkle Trees, enabling lightweight verification that inference was executed correctly without rerunning the model. Existing verifiable inference approaches either have prohibitive cryptographic overhead (too slow for LLM scale) or require the verifier to possess a strong GPU, making client-side verification impractical for users accessing cloud LLM services. Useful for: Cloud LLM inference services where users cannot trust the provider’s execution integrity
Vault connections: GPU optimization for LLM inference, Batch Verification in Speculative Decoding, LLM Inference Performance: The Team Mental Model + Experiment Playbook


🎯 Active Projects

2. Visual RAG Toolkit: Scaling Multi-Vector Visual Retrieval with Training-Free Pooling and Multi-Stage Search
Summary:
Reduce multi-vector visual retrieval costs by applying static spatial pooling over patch embeddings to create compact tile-level and global representations for fast candidate generation, followed by exact MaxSim reranking with full embeddings—achieving quadratic reduction in comparisons without requiring post-training. Multi-vector visual retrievers (ColPali-style late interaction models) produce thousands of vectors per page, making indexing and search prohibitively expensive at scale. Useful for: Visual document retrieval systems requiring high accuracy with constrained compute (RAG over scanned documents, PDFs, screenshots)
Deep dive: Full analysis

3. Recurrent Preference Memory for Efficient Long-Sequence Generative Recommendation
Summary:
Compresses long user interaction sequences into compact Preference Memory tokens using self-referential teacher-forcing, enabling parallel training of recurrent models while maintaining constant-time inference complexity. Full-attention generative recommendation models face quadratic computational costs with sequence length and accumulate noise from stochastic interactions, while traditional recurrent methods suffer from slow serial training that prevents efficient scaling. Useful for: Lifelong user modeling in e-commerce and content platforms where users accumulate thousands of interactions
Deep dive: Full analysis

4. Unleashing Low-Bit Inference on Ascend NPUs: A Comprehensive Evaluation of HiFloat Formats
Summary:
A family of hierarchical floating-point formats (HiF8 and HiF4) designed for Ascend NPUs that use block-level scaling factors to preserve precision in high-variance data during LLM inference quantization. Integer quantization (INT4/INT8) suffers from accuracy collapse on high-variance activations and KV-cache data; existing floating-point formats like MXFP and NVFP4 are not optimized for Ascend NPU hardware characteristics. Useful for: LLM weight-only quantization (W4A16, W8A8 configurations)
Deep dive: Full analysis

5. OneVision-Encoder: Codec-Aligned Sparsity as a Foundational Principle for Multimodal Intelligence
Summary:
Align vision encoder architecture with video codec principles by processing only high-entropy regions (3.1%-25% of patches) where predictive residuals concentrate, rather than uniformly processing all pixels. Current vision transformers waste computation on redundant, static background regions instead of focusing on sparse, information-rich areas where motion and semantic changes occur. Useful for: Video understanding tasks (4.1% improvement over dense baselines)
Deep dive: Full analysis

6. Quantization-Robust LLM Unlearning via Low-Rank Adaptation
Summary:
Freeze the base LLM and concentrate machine unlearning updates into trainable LoRA adapters, ensuring that unlearning edits remain effective after aggressive post-training quantization (PTQ) to 4-bit precision. Standard full-parameter fine-tuning for LLM unlearning produces parameter changes too small to survive 4-bit quantization, causing quantized models to revert to pre-unlearning behavior and leak previously “forgotten” knowledge. Useful for: Privacy-compliant LLM deployment requiring GDPR “right to be forgotten” compliance on edge devices or mobile hardware
Deep dive: Full analysis

7. BPDQ: Bit-Plane Decomposition Quantization on a Variable Grid for Large Language Models
Summary:
Construct a variable quantization grid using bit-plane decomposition and scalar coefficients, iteratively refined with second-order information to minimize quantization error, rather than enforcing fixed uniform intervals. Post-training quantization (PTQ) methods deteriorate at 2-3 bits because fixed quantization grids (e.g., UINT2 uniform intervals) severely restrict the feasible set for error minimization, limiting memory-bandwidth-constrained LLM deployments. Useful for: Extremely memory-constrained LLM inference (e.g., serving 72B models on consumer GPUs with 24GB VRAM)
Deep dive: Full analysis

8. SLA2: Sparse-Linear Attention with Learnable Routing and QAT
Summary:
A hybrid attention mechanism that uses a learnable router to dynamically assign each attention computation to either sparse or linear attention branches, combined with a learnable mixing ratio and quantization-aware training to achieve extreme sparsity (97%) while maintaining generation quality in diffusion models. Original SLA uses a suboptimal heuristic (attention-weight magnitude) to split computations between sparse and linear branches, and exhibits a mathematical mismatch between its formulation and the true sparse-linear decomposition; this leads to inefficient routing and degraded quality under high sparsity. Useful for: Video generation models requiring long-context attention (e.g., text-to-video diffusion)
Deep dive: Full analysis

9. Quantized Evolution Strategies: High-precision Fine-tuning of Quantized LLMs at Low-precision Cost
Summary:
A backpropagation-free optimization method that performs full-parameter fine-tuning directly on quantized LLMs by combining accumulated error feedback (to preserve high-precision gradient signals in discrete parameter space) with stateless seed replay (to maintain memory at low-precision inference levels). Standard fine-tuning requires backpropagation through high-precision weights, making it incompatible with Post-Training Quantized (PTQ) models where parameters are discrete and non-differentiable. Useful for: Fine-tuning quantized LLMs on edge/mobile devices with limited memory
Deep dive: Full analysis

10. QuEPT: Quantized Elastic Precision Transformers with One-Shot Calibration for Multi-Bit Switching
Summary:
Enable dynamic multi-bit quantization switching in Transformers through one-shot post-training calibration using cascaded low-rank adapters, eliminating the need for separate optimization per bit-width. Traditional quantization methods require retraining or re-optimization for each target bit-width (2-bit, 3-bit, 4-bit, etc.), making it expensive to deploy models across diverse hardware constraints. Useful for: Deploying LLMs across heterogeneous edge devices with varying memory/compute constraints
Deep dive: Full analysis

11. Quantization-Aware Collaborative Inference for Large Embodied AI Models
Summary:
Jointly optimizes quantization bit-width and computation offloading frequency for large AI models running on resource-constrained embodied agents, using tractable distortion bounds to balance inference quality, latency, and energy consumption. Large AI models (LAIMs) for embodied AI (robots, drones, AR/VR) require massive computation that exceeds edge device capabilities, but naive quantization or offloading strategies either degrade accuracy significantly or violate real-time latency/energy constraints. Useful for: Embodied AI systems: Robotics, autonomous drones, AR/VR headsets running vision-language models (VLMs) or large foundation models
Deep dive: Full analysis

12. FlashSchNet: Fast and Accurate Coarse-Grained Neural Network Molecular Dynamics
Summary:
Accelerate graph neural network molecular dynamics by redesigning SchNet operations to minimize GPU memory transfers through kernel fusion and CSR-based aggregation. GNN-based molecular dynamics potentials (like SchNet) are orders of magnitude slower than classical force fields because they fragment computation into many small kernels that repeatedly read/write intermediate tensors to GPU high-bandwidth memory (HBM), creating a memory-bound bottleneck that underutilizes GPU compute. Useful for: Coarse-grained protein molecular dynamics (269-bead systems achieving 1000 ns/day on single GPU)
Deep dive: Full analysis

digest/2026-02-17.content:
Research Digest — Tue 2026-02-17
Scanned 6 papers • Selected 3 • Filtered 0 off-domain, 1 duplicates • Runtime: 1.8 min

Papers





























#PaperTagsLinks1Can I Have Your Order? Monte-Carlo Tree Search for Slot Filling Ordering in Diffusion Language ModelsDiffusion, Search, RAG, LLMarXiv • Note2SPILLage: Agentic Oversharing on the WebRecSys, RAG, LLMarXiv • Code • Note3DHPLT: large-scale multilingual diachronic corpora and word representations for semantic change modellingRAG, Search, Embeddings, LLMarXiv • Code • Note

Details
1. Can I Have Your Order? Monte-Carlo Tree Search for Slot Filling Ordering in Diffusion Language Models
Source: HuggingFace | Tags: Diffusion, Search, RAG, LLM
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Formulate slot infilling order selection in Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) as a sequential decision-making problem, optimized via Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) with look-ahead simulations to discover orderings that maximize generation quality. Plan-and-infill decoding in MDMs is highly sensitive to the order in which masked slots are filled — different orderings produce high output variance, and naive or sequential heuristics leave significant performance on the table. Useful for: Code generation tasks where sub-routine dependencies make ordering critical (e.g., MBPP benchmarks)
Vault connections: Monte Carlo Tree Search, Context Slotting, Block Sparse Attention

2. SPILLage: Agentic Oversharing on the Web
Source: HuggingFace | Tags: RecSys, RAG, LLM
Code: github.com/jrohsc/SPILLage
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A two-dimensional taxonomy (channel × directness) that formally characterizes how LLM web agents unintentionally disclose task-irrelevant user information through both textual outputs and observable behavioral traces. Prior privacy work on LLM agents focused exclusively on text leakage (what agents type), missing a larger and more insidious class of privacy violation: behavioral signals (clicks, scrolls, navigation) that third parties can monitor to infer sensitive user attributes even when no private text is transmitted. Useful for: Auditing LLM agent deployments that access personal data (email, calendar, health) before acting on the open web
Vault connections: A Survey on LLM-powered Agents for Recommender Systems, The Future is Agentic: Definitions, Perspective, and Open Challenge of Multi-Agent Recommender System, Agentic Reasoning: Main Takeaways and Techniques

3. DHPLT: large-scale multilingual diachronic corpora and word representations for semantic change modelling
Source: HuggingFace | Tags: RAG, Search, Embeddings, LLM
Code: data.hplt-project.org/three/diachronic/
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A large-scale multilingual diachronic corpus collection spanning 41 languages and three time periods, built from web-crawled data with timestamp-based temporal partitioning, paired with pre-computed word embeddings and lexical substitutions for semantic change modelling. The scarcity of multilingual diachronic corpora for semantic change detection — most existing resources cover only a handful of high-resource languages, blocking cross-lingual and typologically diverse studies of how word meanings shift over time. Useful for: Cross-lingual semantic change detection and benchmarking
Vault connections: Infinite Retrieval: Attention Enhanced LLMs in Long-Context Processing, Dense Passage Retrieval in Conversational Search, DocMMIR Cross-Domain Multimodal Benchmark Construction

digest/2026-02-18.content:
Research Digest — Wed 2026-02-18
Scanned 249 papers • Selected 10 • Filtered 0 off-domain, 14 duplicates • Runtime: 3.9 min

Papers







































































#PaperTagsLinks1ER-MIA: Black-Box Adversarial Memory Injection Attacks on Long-Term Memory-Augmented Large Language ModelsLLM, Search, RAG, RecSysarXiv • Note2Sparrow: Text-Anchored Window Attention with Visual-Semantic Glimpsing for Speculative Decoding in Video LLMsLLM, RAGarXiv • Note3Learning to Configure Agentic AI SystemsLLM, RL, Search, RAGarXiv • Note4NeuroSymActive: Differentiable Neural-Symbolic Reasoning with Active Exploration for Knowledge Graph Question AnsweringEmbeddings, LLM, Graph, RAGarXiv • Note5OpaqueToolsBench: Learning Nuances of Tool Behavior Through InteractionLLM, Search, RAGarXiv • Note6ScrapeGraphAI-100k: A Large-Scale Dataset for LLM-Based Web Information ExtractionLLM, Search, RAGarXiv • Note7On Surprising Effectiveness of Masking Updates in Adaptive OptimizersLLMarXiv • Note8Recursive Concept Evolution for Compositional Reasoning in Large Language ModelsLLM, RL, Search, RAGarXiv • Note9ResearchGym: Evaluating Language Model Agents on Real-World AI ResearchLLM, Search, RAGarXiv • Note10Detecting Overflow in Compressed Token Representations for Retrieval-Augmented GenerationLLM, Search, RAGarXiv • Note

Details
1. ER-MIA: Black-Box Adversarial Memory Injection Attacks on Long-Term Memory-Augmented Large Language Models
Source: arXiv:cs.LG | Tags: LLM, Search, RAG, RecSys
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A black-box adversarial framework that exploits similarity-based retrieval mechanisms in long-term memory-augmented LLMs by crafting malicious memory entries that are preferentially retrieved over legitimate ones, hijacking model responses without access to model internals. Long-term memory systems introduce a new attack surface beyond the model itself — the retrieval layer — which is largely undefended and exploitable purely through crafted text inputs, even without white-box access to embeddings or model weights. Useful for: Red-teaming / security auditing: Evaluating robustness of RAG pipelines and memory-augmented agents before deployment.
Vault connections: Infinite Retrieval: Attention Enhanced LLMs in Long-Context Processing, context rot, Adversarial Retrieval Noise Training

2. Sparrow: Text-Anchored Window Attention with Visual-Semantic Glimpsing for Speculative Decoding in Video LLMs
Source: arXiv:cs.AI | Tags: LLM, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A speculative decoding framework for Video LLMs that offloads visual computation entirely to the target model by reusing cached text hidden states in a sliding window attention mechanism, while training the draft model on semantically-enriched intermediate layer states instead of raw visual tokens. Standard speculative decoding collapses on Video LLMs because the draft model suffers from (1) attention dilution — visual KV-cache grows proportionally with video length, drowning out relevant signal — and (2) negative visual gain — processing raw visual tokens in the draft model adds noise rather than useful signal, since by deep inference layers the visual semantics have already been absorbed into text hidden states. Useful for: Real-time long-video understanding (25k+ visual tokens) where standard speculative decoding degrades
Vault connections: LLM Inference Performance: The Team Mental Model + Experiment Playbook, Multimodal Textual Knowledge Generation via Open-Vocabulary VLMs, Block Sparse Attention

3. Learning to Configure Agentic AI Systems
Source: HuggingFace | Tags: LLM, RL, Search, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A hierarchical reinforcement learning policy that dynamically selects per-query agent configurations (workflows, tools, token budgets, prompts) rather than applying a single fixed template to all inputs. Static “one-size-fits-all” agent configurations waste compute on easy queries and underperform on hard ones; hand-tuned heuristics are brittle and fail to exploit the combinatorial configuration space effectively. Useful for: Agentic reasoning systems (tool-augmented QA, multi-step planning) where query difficulty varies widely
Vault connections: Synergistic Tool Integration in Agentic Reasoning, Automated Multi-Agent Architecture Search (ADAS, AFlow, EvoFlow), Agentic Reasoning Pipeline

4. NeuroSymActive: Differentiable Neural-Symbolic Reasoning with Active Exploration for Knowledge Graph Question Answering
Source: arXiv:cs.CL | Tags: Embeddings, LLM, Graph, RAG, Search
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A modular framework that couples differentiable soft-unification symbolic reasoning with a Monte Carlo value-guided exploration controller to perform efficient multi-hop Knowledge Graph Question Answering (KGQA). Integrating knowledge graph structure with neural models is caught between two failure modes — embedding raw graph facts into prompts is inefficient and brittle, while purely symbolic/search-heavy traversal is expensive in graph lookups and lacks gradient-based refinement. Useful for: Multi-hop question answering over structured KGs (e.g., Freebase, Wikidata, domain ontologies)
Vault connections: KAPING, GraphToken, Multi-source Knowledge Line Graph Prompting (MKLGP)

5. OpaqueToolsBench: Learning Nuances of Tool Behavior Through Interaction
Source: arXiv:cs.CL | Tags: LLM, Search, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Iteratively refines tool documentation by observing execution feedback from tool-calling trajectories, enabling LLM agents to self-improve their understanding of opaque tools without expensive human annotation. Real-world tools are often underspecified—lacking clear best practices, edge case behavior, and failure modes—making existing LLM benchmarks (which assume perfectly documented tools) poor proxies for actual deployment. Useful for: LLM agents operating against third-party or legacy APIs with poor documentation
Vault connections: Synergistic Tool Integration in Agentic Reasoning, Agentic Reasoning Design Patterns, Dynamic Tool Invocation

6. ScrapeGraphAI-100k: A Large-Scale Dataset for LLM-Based Web Information Extraction
Source: arXiv:cs.IR | Tags: LLM, Search, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A real-world, large-scale dataset of LLM-based structured web extraction events is constructed from opt-in telemetry, then deduplicated and schema-balanced to produce high-quality training and benchmark examples. Existing web information extraction datasets are small, synthetic, or text-only, lacking structural context (schemas, JSON outputs, validation metadata) needed to train or benchmark LLMs on realistic structured extraction tasks. Useful for: Fine-tuning small language models (1–3B) for structured extraction, narrowing the gap to much larger models (30B+)
Vault connections: Millions of GeAR: Extending GraphRAG to Millions of Document, Multi-Partite Graph Index, Graph-Enhanced RAG

7. On Surprising Effectiveness of Masking Updates in Adaptive Optimizers
Source: arXiv:cs.AI | Tags: LLM
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Selectively mask parameter updates based on alignment between current gradients and momentum, inducing curvature-aware geometric regularization that smooths optimization trajectories. Dense adaptive optimizers (Adam, Muon) apply updates to all parameters at every step, accumulating noise along high-curvature directions and making optimization trajectories erratic; existing methods address this with increasingly complex preconditioners at significant computational cost. Useful for: LLM pre-training at scale (demonstrated 1B+ parameter models with >19% perplexity reduction vs. Adam)
Vault connections: Large Language Models as Optimizers, Saliency-Aware Pruning with Learnable Masks, Adaptive Low-Precision Training with Mixed Precision Caching

8. Recursive Concept Evolution for Compositional Reasoning in Large Language Models
Source: arXiv:cs.CL | Tags: LLM, RL, Search, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
RCE enables pretrained LLMs to dynamically construct new low-rank representational subspaces during inference when existing latent geometry is insufficient to support a required abstraction. LLMs hit a hard ceiling on compositional reasoning tasks (ARC-AGI-2, GPQA, MATH, BBH, HLE) because their representation space is fixed at inference time — when a required abstraction isn’t already encoded, the model cannot construct it, causing sharp accuracy degradation that token-level search methods (chain-of-thought, self-consistency, RL) cannot fix. Useful for: Compositional and multi-step reasoning tasks that require building new abstractions from primitives (ARC-AGI-2, math olympiad problems)
Vault connections: Reasoning Beyond Limits: Advances and Open Problems for LLMs, Compositional Reasoning Retrieval Evaluation, Take a Step Back: Evoking Reasoning via Abstraction in Large Language Model

9. ResearchGym: Evaluating Language Model Agents on Real-World AI Research
Source: arXiv:cs.AI | Tags: LLM, Search, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
A containerized benchmark environment that evaluates AI agents on end-to-end scientific research by providing baseline implementations from real published papers while withholding the proposed methods, requiring agents to independently discover and surpass state-of-the-art solutions. Existing agent benchmarks test narrow, synthetic tasks rather than the full research loop (hypothesis generation → experimentation → evaluation), making it impossible to measure whether agents can perform genuine open-ended scientific discovery in realistic computational environments. Useful for: Benchmarking frontier AI agents on realistic long-horizon scientific tasks
Vault connections: Taxonomy-Guided Benchmarking for Agentic Evaluation, Agentic Reasoning Design Patterns, Language Agents by Yu Su

10. Detecting Overflow in Compressed Token Representations for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
Source: HuggingFace | Tags: LLM, Search, RAG
Deep dive: Full analysis
Summary:
Train lightweight probing classifiers over joint query and compressed-context representations to detect when soft token compression has discarded information necessary to answer a query, enabling pre-LLM gating before inference. Soft compression architectures (e.g., xRAG) reduce token sequences to compact learned representations, but provide no signal about whether a given query’s answer survives compression — leading to silent failures where the LLM confidently generates wrong answers from information-lossy inputs. Useful for: RAG pipelines with soft compression where latency savings must be balanced against answer reliability
Vault connections: Infinite Retrieval: Attention Enhanced LLMs in Long-Context Processing, Context Compression, Lengthy Context Retrieval via LLM Pretraining
